summary: Matched the ftCo_DownDamage file at 100%, moving six down-damage-related routines out of asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s into a new C translation unit and header under ftCommon. The PR added the new object to obj_files.mk, introduced a downdamage motion-vars struct for fp+2340, typed ftCommonData.x428 as int to support the percent-threshold comparison, and cleaned ft_08A4.h declarations so ftCo_8009F0F0 lives in ftCo_DownDamage.h while ftCo_8009F184 remains file-local.
what_changed: Removed the previously hand-written assembly for ftCo_8009F0F0, ftCo_8009F184, ftCo_DownDamage_Anim, ftCo_DownDamage_IASA, ftCo_DownDamage_Phys, and ftCo_DownDamage_Coll from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DownDamage.c with matched C implementations for the DownDamage state logic.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DownDamage.h declaring ftCo_8009F0F0 and the DownDamage callbacks.
Added ftCo_DownDamage.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk before the remaining ft_08A4.s object.
Included ftCo_DownDamage.h from ftCo_Damage.c so the newly split declarations are visible where damage code needs them.
Added union ftCommon_MotionVars.downdamage with float x0 at fp+2340, matching the animation countdown storage used by ftCo_DownDamage_Anim.
Changed ftCommonData.x428 from UNK_T to int, matching its use as an integer common-data threshold converted to double/float for comparison against dmg.x1838_percentTemp.
Removed ftCo_8009F0F0 and ftCo_8009F184 declarations from ft_08A4.h; ftCo_8009F184 became static in the new C file.
smart_moves: Kept the helper ftCo_8009F184 file-local with a static forward declaration, avoiding exporting a routine only used by ftCo_DownDamage.c.
Modeled fp+2340 through a named motion-vars union member, fp->mv.co.downdamage.x0, instead of leaving raw offsets or borrowing unrelated motion-var names.
Typed p_ftCommonData->x428 as int to match the assembly's integer load and xoris/lfd conversion pattern before comparing to percentTemp.

Used existing fighter helpers and state-transition functions rather than duplicating behavior: ftCo_8008DCE0, ftCommon_8007E2F4, ftCo_Damage_Phys, ftCo_8008FC94, ftCo_80098160, ftCo_80097F38, ftCo_80090780, and ftCo_800CC730.
Encoded the up/down DownDamage motion-state choice directly with FtMotionId ternaries, preserving the assembly's DownWaitU to DownDamageU and DownDamageU to DownStandU relationships.
Preserved empty IASA behavior as an empty C function, matching the one-instruction blr routine.
Used RETURN_IF around helper calls whose return values were only used for control flow in the matched assembly.
decomp_lessons: When matching fighter state files, split newly matched state callbacks into a dedicated ftCo_<State>.c/.h pair and remove only the corresponding asm block from the larger asm file.
Motion-var fields at fp+2340 should be represented in ftCommon_MotionVars with a state-specific sub-struct when evidence identifies the owning state.
Assembly integer-to-floating conversion sequences can reveal that an unknown common-data field is an int even if the comparison target is float.
A trivial physics callback that only calls another physics routine can often match as a thin wrapper with the same gobj argument.
Empty action-state callbacks should be kept as explicit empty functions when the original asm is just blr.
For collision callbacks, preserve the branch shape with early-return macros when helper return values determine whether later collision handlers execute.
naming_conventions: New common fighter state source/header follows ftCo_DownDamage.c and ftCo_DownDamage.h naming.
Action-state callbacks use the suffixes _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, and _Coll.
Unidentified address-based functions retained ftCo_8009F0F0 naming, while ftCo_8009F184 was made static because it is an internal helper.
State-specific motion vars are named under fp->mv.co.downdamage, with unknown fields kept as x0.
Motion IDs use ftCo_MS_DownBoundU, ftCo_MS_DownWaitU, ftCo_MS_DownDamageU, ftCo_MS_DownBoundD, ftCo_MS_DownWaitD, ftCo_MS_DownDamageD, ftCo_MS_DownStandU, and ftCo_MS_DownStandD.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched a six-function slice by deleting 214 lines of asm and replacing it with 98 lines of C plus precise header and type updates.
Translated the assembly's motion_id comparisons against 0xB7, 0xB8, 0xB9 and range check for 0xBF through 0xC1 into explicit DownBound/DownWait/DownDamage motion ID checks.
Represented bit extraction from fp+0x2224 as the existing bitfield fp->x2224_b2.
Mapped fp+0x2340 decrement and threshold check to fp->mv.co.downdamage.x0 -= 1 and <= 0.
Mapped fp+0xE0 ground/air checks to fp->ground_or_air == GA_Air or GA_Ground.
Mapped collision flag tests from coll_data offsets to coll->env_flags & MPCOLL_FLAGS_B11 and MPCOLL_FLAGS_B05.
Used GET_FIGHTER(gobj) in animation logic and direct gobj->user_data where that better matched local variable usage.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text excerpt is a matching report showing the file and all listed functions at 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftCo_8009F0F0 or ftCo_8009F184 to semantic DownDamage helper names.
Search for other uses of ftCommonData.x428 to confirm its semantic name and units.
Search for other state-specific fp+2340 motion-var structs to align naming and field semantics with downdamage.x0.
Inspect later ft_08A4.s splits around ftCo_8009F39C and ftCo_8009F75C for continuation of this decomp area.
